content:
So I need something like this:

  1. Run: $tool $message-id-from-ml --reply (or --save $fs-path)

  2. Opens the message in vim

  3. Sends or saves the message (depending or the given flag) if changes are saved on exit (:wq / :wq!), or drops it on discard (:q / :q!).

Does anyone have a wrapper tool like this? Otherwise, I will build one myself. It would make interacting with patches much faster and easier for me.

Right now it's very time consuming to work with on patches which are not sent to my inbox (means almost any patches since I am not in the MAINTAINERS file).
https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260403123654.155249-1-work@onurozkan.dev/ was sent using that tool with mreply --reply https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260313091646.16938-1-work@onurozkan.dev/raw
I use aerc so I just :import-mbox <lore URL>/raw and reply to that email

Gary Guo said:

I use aerc so I just :import-mbox <lore URL>/raw and reply to that email

Nice, the one I just made is not an e-mail client. It's using git send-email for sending
@Benno Lossin also had a fancy system too, I think.

it looks like you might want to use lei to get emails published on a list you are not subscribed to directly into a local inbox - that way you can use any MUA (including something vim-based) to read them and reply.
Yeah, but I didn't wanted to use multiple things to do that
What I was looking was a single command to send reply

Miguel Ojeda said:

Benno Lossin also had a fancy system too, I think.

yes, it stores all the mail in a local dir (currently via lei, but I wanted to just use wget) and then opens your mail client there
